---
title: "PAPER"
author: "Edith"
date: "2026-01-30"
output: DATA ANALISES
---

OB.1: Cuantificar la variación en la riqueza, abundancia y composición de epífitas vasculares a lo largo del gradiente elevacional hacia el treeline.

Data Epis treeline
```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/edith clemente arena/Desktop/EPIFITAS -GRADIENTE/treeline-epiphytes/treeline-epiphytes")
#FOROFITOS
data <- read.csv("estrucPOB.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
str(data)
```
paquetes
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(car)
library(stringi)
library(lme4)
library(performance)
library(emmeans)
library(sjmisc)
library(sjPlot)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(betapart)
library(MASS)
library(patchwork)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
library(emmeans)
library(ggpubr)

```

riqueza
```{r}
#Crear matriz de abundancia
comm <- data %>%
  group_by(forof, elevation, morphospe) %>%
  summarise(abundance = n(), .groups="drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = morphospe, values_from = abundance, values_fill = 0)
#Matriz ambiental del árbol
env_tree <- data %>%
  distinct(forof, elevation, dap, height, plot, subplot, tree_name, genera_tree)
#RIQUEZA DE EPÍFITAS POR ÁRBOL
species_cols <- names(comm)[!names(comm) %in% c("forof","elevation")]
#datos raros nombres
data$morphospe <- stri_trans_general(data$morphospe, "Latin-ASCII")
data$morphospe <- gsub("[^[:alnum:] ]", "", data$morphospe)

richness <- comm %>%
  mutate(richness = rowSums(across(all_of(species_cols), ~ . > 0))) %>%
  dplyr::select(forof, elevation, richness)

#MODELOS A LO LARGO DEL GRADIENTE ELEVACIONAL
#Riqueza mejor fit con elevacion como factor y hacer anova con grafico de boxplot con resultados de pairwise. 20FEB
str(richness)
richness$elev_f <- factor(richness$elevation)

m_rich <- glmer(richness ~ elev_f + (1|forof),
                family=poisson, data=richness)
summary(m_rich)
Anova(m_rich)
check_model(m_rich)###valuar de forma rápida y visual si un modelo estadístico está bien especificado y si cumple los supuestos.1. linealidad, 2. homocedasticidad, 3. Normalidad de residuos, 4. Valores atipicos e influencia, 4. Colinearidad, Ajuste general del modelo
library(performance)##cuando no muestra el plot hace otra ventana
dev.new()
check_model(m_rich)
#comparacion por pares
emmeans(m_rich, list(pairwise ~ elev_f), adjust = "tukey")
#revisar overdispersion
check_overdispersion(m_rich)#sin overdispercion

#regresion
richness$elev_z <- scale(richness$elevation)
m_richz <- glmer(richness ~ elev_z + (1|forof),
                family=poisson, data=richness)
summary(m_richz)
Anova(m_richz)
plot(richness$elevation, richness$richness)
check_model(m_richz)#
plot(m_richz)
#comparacion por pares
emmeans(m_richz, list(pairwise ~ elev_z), adjust = "tukey")
plot_model(m_richz,
type = "pred",
terms = "elev_z")

###La elevación se trató como una variable categórica debido a que solo se muestrearon cuatro niveles discretos, lo que impide el uso de enfoques de suavizamiento continuo como los GAM
#grafico
riq_el<- ggplot(richness, aes(elev_f, richness)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width=0.1, alpha=0.4) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab("Epiphyte species richness per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)")
```
RIQUEZA grafico completo con comparaciones
```{r}
#Extraer las comparaciones de emmeans como data frame
emm <- emmeans(m_rich, ~ elev_f)
pairs_df <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey") %>%
  as.data.frame()
#data frame para ggplot
pairs_df <- pairs_df %>%
  separate(contrast, into = c("group1", "group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = gsub("elev_f", "", group1),
    group2 = gsub("elev_f", "", group2),
    y.position = max(richness$richness) + 
                 seq(0.2, by = 0.2, length.out = n())
  )

#GRAFICO con asteriscos
colnames(pairs_df)
pairs_df <- pairs_df %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  )
riq_el <- ggplot(richness, aes(elev_f, richness)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.4) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pairs_df,
    label = "p.signif",
    tip.length = 0.01
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab("Epiphyte species richness per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)")
riq_el
#corregir visualmente
pairs_sig <- pairs_df %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.05)
pairs_sig <- pairs_sig %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = max(richness$richness, na.rm = TRUE) +
                 seq(2, by = 2, length.out = n())
  )
riq_el <- ggplot(richness, aes(elev_f, richness)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pairs_sig,
    label = "p.signif",
    tip.length = 0.01
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  ylab("Epiphyte species richness per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)")
riq_el
#caption grafico: Boxes show median and interquartile range. Points represent individual trees.Asterisks indicate significant pairwise differences among elevations based on Tukey-adjusted estimated marginal means derived from the GLMM (* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01).
```

ABUNDANCIA
```{r}
#ABUNDANCIA TOTAL
abundance <- comm %>%
  mutate(abundance = rowSums(dplyr::select(., -forof, -elevation))) %>%
  dplyr::select(forof, elevation, abundance)
#Abundancia 
abund <- data %>%
  group_by(forof, elevation) %>%
  summarise(abundance = n(), .groups="drop")
#modelo 
m_abund <- glmer(abundance ~ elev_f + (1|forof),
                 family = poisson, data = abund)
summary(m_abund)
#modelo elegido###################################
abundance$elev_f <- factor(abundance$elevation)

m_abun <- glmer(abundance ~ elev_f + (1|forof),
                family=poisson, data=abundance)
summary(m_abun)
Anova(m_abun)

check_model(m_abun)###valuar de forma rápida y visual si un modelo estadístico está bien especificado y si cumple los supuestos.1. linealidad, 2. homocedasticidad, 3. Normalidad de residuos, 4. Valores atipicos e influencia, 4. Colinearidad, Ajuste general del modelo
#comparacion por pares
library(performance)##cuando no muestra el plot hace otra ventana
dev.new()
check_model(m_abun)
#revisar overdispersion
check_overdispersion(m_abun)#sin overdispercion
emmeans(m_abun, list(pairwise ~ elev_f), adjust = "tukey")#comparando pares

#regresion PEOR AJUSTE
abundance$elev_z <- scale(abundance$elevation)
abundance$elev_z <- as.numeric(abundance$elev_z)
m_abunz <- glmer(abundance ~ elev_z + (1|forof),
                family=poisson, data=abundance)
summary(m_abunz)
Anova(m_abunz)
check_model(m_abunz)#
library(performance)##cuando no muestra el plot hace otra ventana
dev.new()
check_model(m_abunz)

#grafico solo
ggplot(abundance, aes(elev_f, abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width=0.1, alpha=0.4) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab("Epiphyte abundance per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)")
```
Grafico ABUNDANCE completo con las comparaciones
```{r}
############################################
## MODELO DE ABUNDANCIA (GLMM POISSON)
############################################
m_abun <- glmer(
  abundance ~ elev_f + (1 | forof),
  family = poisson,
  data = abundance
)

############################################
## EMMEANS Y COMPARACIONES (Tukey)
############################################
emm_abun <- emmeans(m_abun, ~ elev_f)
pairs_abun <- pairs(emm_abun, adjust = "tukey") |> as.data.frame()

# (solo informativo, no se usa más abajo)
pairs_abun
# Comparación significativa:
# elev_f3402 - elev_f3537  p = 0.0001

############################################
## GRÁFICO FINAL CON ANOTACIÓN MANUAL
############################################

# Posiciones X de los niveles del factor:
# levels(abundance$elev_f)
# 1 = 3261
# 2 = 3402
# 3 = 3537
# 4 = 3625

abun_el <- ggplot(abundance, aes(elev_f, abundance)) +

  # boxplots y puntos
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +

  # línea horizontal de comparación (3402 vs 3537)
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 2, xend = 3, y = 450, yend = 450),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # "patitas" verticales
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 2, xend = 2, y = 420, yend = 450),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 3, xend = 3, y = 420, yend = 450),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # asteriscos de significancia
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2.5, y = 480,
    label = "***",
    size = 6
  ) +

  # escala log SOLO visual
  scale_y_log10() +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 650)) +

  # estética final
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  ylab("Epiphyte abundance per tree (log scale)") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)")

abun_el
#caption : Boxplots show epiphyte abundance per tree (log scale). Asterisks indicate a significant pairwise difference based on Tukey-adjusted estimated marginal means from a Poisson GLMM.
```

Comparando el efecto de la elevacion con la riq y abund
```{r}
#glm incluidos en los resultados 18FEB que capta la variablilidad de los forofitos diferentes no todos son iguales y asi no sobrestima la variabilidad de la elevacion 
richness$elev_z <- scale(richness$elevation)
str(richness)
m_rich_z <- glmer(richness ~ elev_z + (1|forof),
                  family = poisson, data = richness)
abundance$elev_z <- scale(abundance$elevation)
m_abund_z <- glmer(abundance ~ elev_z + (1|forof),
                   family = poisson, data = abundance)
summary(m_rich_z)
summary(m_abund_z)
#####GLM con eleva sin efecto aleatorio
m_rich2 <- glm(richness ~ elev_z,
                  family = poisson, data = richness)
summary(m_rich2)
m_abund2 <- glm(abundance ~ elev_z,
                   family = poisson, data = abundance)
summary(m_abund2)

m_rich3 <- glm(richness ~ elevation,
                  family = poisson, data = richness)
summary(m_rich3)
m_abund3 <- glm(abundance ~ elevation,
                   family = poisson, data = abundance)
summary(m_abund3)
#sobredispersion 
check_overdispersion(m_rich_z)
check_overdispersion(m_abund_z)
#grafico riq y abund
pA <- ggplot(richness, aes(elev_f, richness)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab("Species richness per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)") +
  labs(tag = "A")
pB <- ggplot(abund, aes(elev_f, abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_classic() +
  ylab("Abundance per tree") +
  xlab("Elevation (m)") +
  labs(tag = "B")
p_compuesto <- pA + pB
ggsave(
  filename = "Fig_richness_abundance elevation .png",
  plot = p_compuesto,
  width = 18,
  height = 10,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 300
)


```
Composición
```{r}
#COMPOSICIÓN DE ESPECIES
# Matriz de abundancia por árbol
#Separar metadata y matriz de especies
meta <- comm %>% dplyr::select(forof, elevation)
comm_mat <- comm %>% dplyr::select(-forof, -elevation)
###################nombres raros
# reemplazar el caracter raro por espacio
colnames(comm_mat) <- gsub("�", " ", colnames(comm_mat))
# quitar dobles espacios
colnames(comm_mat) <- gsub(" +", " ", colnames(comm_mat))
# limpiar espacios al inicio y final
colnames(comm_mat) <- trimws(colnames(comm_mat))
###########################################
# Convertir a matriz
comm_mat <- as.matrix(comm_mat)
rownames(comm_mat) <- meta$forof

#Transformación recomendada (muy importante) Epífitas tienen muchos ceros → usa Hellinger (estándar en ecología)

comm_hel <- decostand(comm_mat, method = "hellinger")
#NMDS
set.seed(123)
nmds <- metaMDS(comm_hel, distance = "chao", k = 2, trymax = 100)
nmds

#grafico set.seed(123)
scores_nmds <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds, display = "sites"))
scores_nmds$elevation <- factor(meta$elevation)
ggplot(scores_nmds, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, color = elevation)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(color = "Elevation (m)", title = "NMDS of epiphyte community composition")

#PERMANOVA 
meta$elev_f <- factor(meta$elevation)
adonis_res <- adonis2(comm_hel ~ elev_f, data = meta, method = "bray", permutations = 999)
adonis_res
##test de dispersion
disp <- betadisper(vegdist(comm_hel, method="chao"), meta$elev_f)
anova(disp)

#TURNOVER (beta diversity)
meta <- comm %>% 
  dplyr::select(forof, elevation) %>% 
  distinct()
meta <- meta[match(rownames(comm_mat), meta$forof), ]
meta$elev_f <- factor(meta$elevation)
dist_chao <- vegdist(comm_mat, method="chao")
beta <- betadisper(dist_bray, meta$elev_f)
anova(beta)

#conclusion: La composición cambia con la elevación, pero también cambia la heterogeneidad entre forófitos.

#grafico 
boxplot(beta, 
        xlab = "Elevation (m)", 
        ylab = "Distance to centroid (chao)")

#turnover y nestedness
#matriz-ausencia

# comm_mat = matriz especies (186 x spp)
comm_pa <- comm_mat
comm_pa[comm_pa > 0] <- 1
# Beta diversity partitioning (Sørensen)
beta_core <- betapart.core(comm_pa)
beta_pair <- beta.pair(beta_core, index.family = "sorensen")

# Componentes
beta_sim <- beta_pair$beta.sim   # turnover
beta_sne <- beta_pair$beta.sne   # nestedness
beta_sor <- beta_pair$beta.sor   # beta total

#MULTI-SITE BETA (valores globales para Results)
beta_multi <- beta.multi(comm_pa, index.family = "sorensen")
beta_multi

# PERMANOVA por elevBETA TOTAL
adonis_sor <- adonis2(beta_sor ~ elev_f, data = meta, permutations = 999)
adonis_sor

#grafico
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
beta_df <- data.frame(
  turnover = as.vector(as.dist(beta_sim)),
  nestedness = as.vector(as.dist(beta_sne)),
  elev = meta$elev_f[combn(1:nrow(meta),2)[1,]]
)
ggplot(beta_df, aes(elev, turnover)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  ylab("Turnover (βSIM)")


summary(m_rich)
summary(m_abund)
beta_multi
```


OB.2: Evaluar la influencia de la estructura (DAP y altura) y la identidad taxonómica (género) del árbol hospedero sobre la riqueza y abundancia de epífitas, controlando el efecto de la elevación.

base datos: ojo todos los arboles
```{r}
#########primero la base de datos que posee a todos los arboles incleyendo loos que no tuvieron epifitas
setwd("C:/Users/edith clemente arena/Desktop/EPIFITAS -GRADIENTE/treeline-epiphytes/treeline-epiphytes")
#FOROFITOS
data <- read.csv("estrucPOB.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
hospepi <- data %>% ###TABLA DE DATOS POBLACIONALES
  dplyr::select(plot, elevation, forof,morphospe, family, genera_tree, dap, height)
str(hospepi)
#ARBOLES
trees <- read.csv("treealtu.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
tree <- trees %>% ###TABLA DE DATOS POBLACIONALES
  dplyr::select(plot, elevation, tagtree,family, genus, NC, DBH, H)
str(tree)

#resumir epifitas por forofito
epi_por_arbol <- hospepi %>%
  group_by(forof, plot, elevation, genera_tree, dap, height) %>%
  summarise(
    presencia_epifitas = 1,
    riqueza = n_distinct(morphospe),
    abundancia_total = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#unificar nombres tree
tree2 <- tree %>%
  rename(
    forof = tagtree,
    genera_tree = genus,
    dap = DBH,
    height = H
  )
epi_por_arbol <- hospepi %>%
  group_by(forof) %>%
  summarise(
    presencia_epifitas = 1,
    riqueza = n_distinct(morphospe),
    abundancia_total = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#unir tablas 
arboles <- tree2 %>%
  left_join(epi_por_arbol, by = "forof") %>%
  mutate(
    presencia_epifitas = ifelse(is.na(presencia_epifitas), 0, presencia_epifitas),
    riqueza = ifelse(is.na(riqueza), 0, riqueza),
    abundancia_total = ifelse(is.na(abundancia_total), 0, abundancia_total)
  )
nrow(arboles)                 # debe ser 193
table(arboles$presencia_epifitas)
table(arboles$plot, arboles$presencia_epifitas)#para ver cuantos arboles estan con epifitas

# riqueza 
arboles$genera_tree <- as.factor(arboles$genera_tree)
arboles$plot <- as.factor(arboles$plot)#factores
riqueza_arbol <- hospepi %>%
  group_by(forof, plot, elevation, genera_tree, dap, height) %>%
  summarise(
    riqueza = n_distinct(morphospe),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
str(riqueza_arbol)

#abundancia
abund_arbol <- hospepi %>%
  group_by(forof, plot, elevation, genera_tree, dap, height) %>%
  summarise(
    abundancia = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
str(abund_arbol)

```
abundancia/riqueza => caracteristicas hospedero
```{r}
#preparacion de datos escalar var numericas y genero como factor 
riqueza_arbol$genera_tree <- as.factor(riqueza_arbol$genera_tree)
abund_arbol$genera_tree  <- as.factor(abund_arbol$genera_tree)
riqueza_arbol$dap_z     <- scale(riqueza_arbol$dap)
riqueza_arbol$height_z  <- scale(riqueza_arbol$height)
riqueza_arbol$elev_z    <- scale(riqueza_arbol$elevation)

abund_arbol$dap_z       <- scale(abund_arbol$dap)
abund_arbol$height_z    <- scale(abund_arbol$height)
abund_arbol$elev_z      <- scale(abund_arbol$elevation)
#Modelo para riqueza de epífitas
#poison
m_ric_pois <- glm(
  riqueza ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  family = poisson,
  data = riqueza_arbol
)
summary(m_ric_pois)
check_overdispersion(m_ric_pois)##confirmada la sobredispersion
##modelo Binomial negativo sugerido 
m_ric_nb <- glm.nb(
  riqueza ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  data = riqueza_arbol
)
summary(m_ric_nb)
Anova(m_ric_nb, type = "II")#importancia de cada variable 
#Modelo para abundancia de epífitas
#poison
m_ab_pois <- glm(
  abundancia ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  family = poisson,
  data = abund_arbol
)
check_overdispersion(m_ab_pois)##confirmada la sobredispersion
#modelo binomial negativo obligatoriamente
m_ab_nb <- glm.nb(
  abundancia ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  data = abund_arbol
)
summary(m_ab_nb)
Anova(m_ab_nb, type = "II")

#modelos solo el efecto del hospedero
m_ricnb_h <- glm.nb(
  riqueza ~ dap_z + height_z  + (1/elev_z),
  data = riqueza_arbol
)
m_abnb_h <- glm.nb(
  abundancia ~ dap_z + height_z + (1/elev_z),
  data = abund_arbol
)
summary(m_ricnb_h)
summary(m_abnb_h)
```
CHEQUEANDO COLINEARIDAD 
resulto que tuen valores de 1,8 lo cual es bajo y no hay problema de colinearidad
```{r}
cor(riqueza_arbol[,c("dap","height","elevation")],
    use = "complete.obs")
names(riqueza_arbol)

riqueza_arbol$riqueza <- as.numeric(riqueza_arbol$riqueza)
str(riqueza_arbol$riqueza)
m_vif_rich <- lm(riqueza ~ dap_z + height_z + elev_z,
                 data = riqueza_arbol)
vif(m_vif_rich)
m_vif_abund <- lm(abundancia ~ dap_z + height_z + elev_z,
                  data = abund_arbol)
vif(m_vif_abund)


```

graficos
```{r}
#Gráfico 1. Riqueza de epífitas vs DAP del hospedero
#preparacion de datos
#variables como numericas
riqueza_arbol <- riqueza_arbol %>%
  mutate(
    dap_z = as.numeric(scale(dap)),
    height_z = as.numeric(scale(height)),
    elev_z = as.numeric(scale(elevation))
  )
#ajustar el modelo binomial negativo
m_ric_nb <- glm.nb(
  riqueza ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  data = riqueza_arbol
)
#dataframe
new_dap <- data.frame(
  dap_z = seq(min(riqueza_arbol$dap_z),
              max(riqueza_arbol$dap_z),
              length.out = 100),
  height_z = 0,   # valor promedio
  elev_z   = 0,   # valor promedio
  genera_tree = levels(riqueza_arbol$genera_tree)[1]
)

#predicciones del modelo
pred <- predict(
  m_ric_nb,
  newdata = new_dap,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)
new_dap <- new_dap %>%
  mutate(
    fit = exp(pred$fit),
    lwr = exp(pred$fit - 1.96 * pred$se.fit),
    upr = exp(pred$fit + 1.96 * pred$se.fit)
  )

#grafico  Riqueza DAP
ggplot(riqueza_arbol, aes(x = dap, y = riqueza)) +
  # datos observados
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2, color = "black") +
  # línea del modelo
  geom_line(
    data = new_dap,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = dap_z * sd(riqueza_arbol$dap) + mean(riqueza_arbol$dap),
      y = fit
    ),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  # intervalo de confianza
  geom_ribbon(
    data = new_dap,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = dap_z * sd(riqueza_arbol$dap) + mean(riqueza_arbol$dap),
      ymin = lwr,
      ymax = upr
    ),
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "DAP del árbol hospedero (cm)",
    y = "Riqueza de epífitas vasculares"
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 13)
ggsave(
  filename = "Fig_Riqueza_DAP.png",
  width = 18,      # ancho en cm
  height = 14,     # alto en cm
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 300
)

#Gráfico 2. Abundancia de epífitas vs DAP y altura
abund_arbol <- abund_arbol %>%#preparacion de datos
  mutate(
    dap_z    = as.numeric(scale(dap)),
    height_z = as.numeric(scale(height)),
    elev_z   = as.numeric(scale(elevation)),
    genera_tree = as.factor(genera_tree)
  )
m_ab_nb <- glm.nb(#ajustar modelo
  abundancia ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  data = abund_arbol
)
#prediccioneds Dap
new_dap_ab <- data.frame(
  dap_z = seq(min(abund_arbol$dap_z),
              max(abund_arbol$dap_z),
              length.out = 100),
  height_z = 0,
  elev_z   = 0,
  genera_tree = levels(abund_arbol$genera_tree)[1]
)

pred_dap <- predict(
  m_ab_nb,
  newdata = new_dap_ab,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

new_dap_ab <- new_dap_ab %>%
  mutate(
    fit = exp(pred_dap$fit),
    lwr = exp(pred_dap$fit - 1.96 * pred_dap$se.fit),
    upr = exp(pred_dap$fit + 1.96 * pred_dap$se.fit)
  )
#predicciones para altura
new_h_ab <- data.frame(
  height_z = seq(min(abund_arbol$height_z),
                 max(abund_arbol$height_z),
                 length.out = 100),
  dap_z  = 0,
  elev_z = 0,
  genera_tree = levels(abund_arbol$genera_tree)[1]
)

pred_h <- predict(
  m_ab_nb,
  newdata = new_h_ab,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

new_h_ab <- new_h_ab %>%
  mutate(
    fit = exp(pred_h$fit),
    lwr = exp(pred_h$fit - 1.96 * pred_h$se.fit),
    upr = exp(pred_h$fit + 1.96 * pred_h$se.fit)
  )
#GRAFICO PARTIDO
#A DAP
p_dap <- ggplot(abund_arbol, aes(x = dap, y = abundancia)) +

  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2, color = "black") +

  geom_line(
    data = new_dap_ab,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = dap_z * sd(abund_arbol$dap) + mean(abund_arbol$dap),
      y = fit
    ),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  geom_ribbon(
    data = new_dap_ab,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = dap_z * sd(abund_arbol$dap) + mean(abund_arbol$dap),
      ymin = lwr,
      ymax = upr
    ),
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "DBH Host tree (cm)",
    y = "Abundance"
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 13)
#B ALTURA
p_h <- ggplot(abund_arbol, aes(x = height, y = abundancia)) +

  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2, color = "black") +

  geom_line(
    data = new_h_ab,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = height_z * sd(abund_arbol$height) + mean(abund_arbol$height),
      y = fit
    ),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  geom_ribbon(
    data = new_h_ab,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = height_z * sd(abund_arbol$height) + mean(abund_arbol$height),
      ymin = lwr,
      ymax = upr
    ),
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Height (m)",
    y = "Abundance"
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 13)
p_dap + p_h#UNIR LOS GRAFICOS

ggsave(
  filename = "Fig_Abundancia_DAP y altura.png",
  width = 18,      # ancho en cm
  height = 14,     # alto en cm
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 300
)

#OPCION B UN SOLO GRAFICO PARA EL DAP Y LA ABUNDANCIA
abund_arbol <- abund_arbol %>%
  mutate(
    dap_z    = as.numeric(scale(dap)),
    height_z = as.numeric(scale(height)),
    elev_z   = as.numeric(scale(elevation)),
    genera_tree = as.factor(genera_tree)
  )
m_ab_nb <- glm.nb(
  abundancia ~ dap_z + height_z + genera_tree + elev_z,
  data = abund_arbol
)
new_dap_ab <- data.frame(
  dap_z = seq(min(abund_arbol$dap_z),
              max(abund_arbol$dap_z),
              length.out = 100),
  height_z = 0,
  elev_z   = 0,
  genera_tree = levels(abund_arbol$genera_tree)[1]
)

pred_dap <- predict(
  m_ab_nb,
  newdata = new_dap_ab,
  type = "link",
  se.fit = TRUE
)

new_dap_ab <- new_dap_ab %>%
  mutate(
    fit = exp(pred_dap$fit),
    lwr = exp(pred_dap$fit - 1.96 * pred_dap$se.fit),
    upr = exp(pred_dap$fit + 1.96 * pred_dap$se.fit)
  )
ggplot(abund_arbol, aes(x = dap, y = abundancia)) +

  # puntos observados (tamaño = altura)
  geom_point(
    aes(size = height),
    alpha = 0.6,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  # línea del modelo
  geom_line(
    data = new_dap_ab,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = dap_z * sd(abund_arbol$dap) + mean(abund_arbol$dap),
      y = fit
    ),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  # intervalo de confianza
  geom_ribbon(
    data = new_dap_ab,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = dap_z * sd(abund_arbol$dap) + mean(abund_arbol$dap),
      ymin = lwr,
      ymax = upr
    ),
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +

  scale_size_continuous(
    name = "Height tree (m)",
    range = c(2, 7)
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "DBH (cm)",
    y = "Abundance"
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
#Guardar la imagen
ggsave(
  filename = "Fig_Abundancia_DAP_altura.png",
  width = 18,      # ancho en cm
  height = 16,     # alto en cm
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 300
)


```


OB.3: Determinar la sobrevivencia de epífitas vasculares translocadas desde el treeline hacia la puna adyacente como prueba experimental de los límites climáticos para su persistencia.

Data SOBREVIVENCIA
```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/edith clemente arena/Desktop/EPIFITAS -GRADIENTE/treeline-epiphytes/treeline-epiphytes")
expdata <- read.csv("relocadata.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
str(expdata)      # Estructura de la base de datos
head(expdata)
colnames(expdata)
#corregir datos extraños
expdata <- expdata %>%
  # Convierte todas las columnas tipo texto a UTF-8
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ stri_encode(.x, from = "", to = "UTF-8"))) %>%
  # Quita espacios en blanco y reemplaza vacíos por NA
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ na_if(trimws(as.character(.x)), "")))
###colocar NA en las celdas vacias
expdata <- expdata %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~iconv(.x, from = "", to = "UTF-8"))) %>%  # 🔧 corrige codificación
  mutate(across(everything(), ~na_if(trimws(.x), "")))  # 🔄 reemplaza vacíos por NA
str(expdata)
colSums(is.na(expdata))

```

Data Clima
```{r}
# 1. Cargar archivo con separador ";"
clima <- read_delim("dataclima.csv",
                    delim = ";",
                    col_types = cols(.default = "c"))  # leer todo como texto primero

# 2. Convertir columnas numéricas correctamente
num_cols <- c("t_puna", "ilum_puna", "t_forest", "ilum_forest",
              "temp_forest", "hr_forest", "temp_puna", "hr_puna")

clima <- clima %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(num_cols), ~ parse_number(.)))

# 3. Convertir la fecha-hora (formato dmy_hm)
clima <- clima %>%
  mutate(
    date_hour = dmy_hm(date_hour),
    date = as.Date(date_hour)
  )

# 4. Crear la tabla diaria
clima_daily <- clima %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(
    light_max_puna   = if (all(is.na(ilum_puna))) NA else max(ilum_puna, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_max_puna    = if (all(is.na(temp_puna))) NA else max(temp_puna, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_min_puna    = if (all(is.na(temp_puna))) NA else min(temp_puna, na.rm = TRUE),
    rh_min_puna      = if (all(is.na(hr_puna)))   NA else min(hr_puna,   na.rm = TRUE),

    light_max_forest = if (all(is.na(ilum_forest))) NA else max(ilum_forest, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_max_forest  = if (all(is.na(temp_forest))) NA else max(temp_forest, na.rm = TRUE),
    temp_min_forest  = if (all(is.na(temp_forest))) NA else min(temp_forest, na.rm = TRUE),
    rh_min_forest    = if (all(is.na(hr_forest)))   NA else min(hr_forest,   na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```
UNIR TABLAS Y CUANTIFICAR LAS VARIABLES EN FUNCION A LOS DATALOGGERS para las evaluaciones
```{r}
head(expdata$date)
expdata$date <- as.Date(expdata$date, format = "%d.%m.%Y")
#Revisar y convertir fechas en tus dos tablas
clima_daily$date <- as.Date(clima_daily$date)
#CLIMA SOLO PUNA
#Crear tabla SOLO clima puna
clima_puna <- clima_daily %>%
  dplyr::select(date, temp_min_puna, temp_max_puna, rh_min_puna, light_max_puna)
#3. Crear tabla con fechas de censos (T1–T4)
census_dates <- expdata %>%
  distinct(time, date) %>%
  arrange(date)

#Calcular clima promedio + extremos por intervalo
climate_intervals <- census_dates %>%
  mutate(start = lag(date),
         end   = date) %>%
  filter(!is.na(start)) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # PROMEDIOS
    Tmin_mean  = mean(clima_puna$temp_min_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE),
    Tmax_mean  = mean(clima_puna$temp_max_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE),
    RHmin_mean = mean(clima_puna$rh_min_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE),
    Light_mean = mean(clima_puna$light_max_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE),

    # EXTREMOS ABSOLUTOS (ecológicamente más importantes)
    Tmin_abs   = min(clima_puna$temp_min_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE),
    RHmin_abs  = min(clima_puna$rh_min_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE),
    Light_abs  = max(clima_puna$light_max_puna[clima_puna$date >= start & clima_puna$date < end], na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
#Unir clima con epífitas
expdata2 <- left_join(expdata, climate_intervals, by = "time")
str(expdata2)
names(expdata2)
#Chequear que T1 no tenga clima
expdata2 %>% distinct(time, Tmin_mean, Tmin_abs)

str(expdata2)
```
```{r}
#Preparar la base SOLO PUNA (porque en bosque no hay variación)
puna_data <- expdata2 %>%
  filter(relocation == "true")   # solo ramas llevadas a puna
names(puna_data)
#Convertir alive a binaria
puna_data$alive <- ifelse(puna_data$dead_alive == "alive", 1,
                          ifelse(puna_data$dead_alive == "dead", 0, NA))
puna_data$alive <- as.integer(puna_data$alive)
table(puna_data$branch_ID, puna_data$time, puna_data$alive)
table(puna_data$alive, puna_data$time)
table(puna_data$branch_ID,puna_data$time)##este era el que vimos

#Escalar clima (muy importante para GLMM)
puna_data <- puna_data %>%
  mutate(
    Tmin_abs_s  = scale(Tmin_abs),
    RHmin_abs_s = scale(RHmin_abs),
    Light_abs_s = scale(Light_abs)
  )
m_both <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + RHmin_abs_s + time + (1|branch_ID),
                family=binomial, data=puna_data)#MODELO ELEGIDO 
```

modelo  sobrevivencia en funcion al clima
```{r}


#Modelo completo
#install.packages("lme4")

m1 <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + RHmin_abs_s + Light_abs_s +
              (1|branch_ID) + (1|genus),
            family = binomial,
            data = puna_data)
summary(m1)
#modelo Solo extremos térmicos e hídricos
m_final <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + RHmin_abs_s + 
                 (1|branch_ID),
                 family=binomial, data=puna_data,
                 control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
summary(m_final)

```
OPCIONES PARA EL MODELO
```{r}
m_temp <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
                family = binomial,
                data = puna_data,
                control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
summary(m_temp)
#GRAFICO
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)

pred <- ggpredict(m_temp, terms = "Tmin_abs_s")

ggplot(pred, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_line(size=1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha=0.2) +
  geom_point(data=puna_data, aes(x=Tmin_abs_s, y=alive), alpha=0.4) +
  labs(x="Minimum absolute temperature (scaled)",
       y="Predicted survival probability") +
  theme_classic()

#Código para comparar AIC DE DIFERENTES MODELOS  
m_rh <- glmer(alive ~ RHmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
              family=binomial, data=puna_data)

str(puna_data)
puna_data$time<-as.factor(puna_data$time)
puna_data$branch_ID<-as.factor(puna_data$branch_ID)
str(puna_data)


m_both <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + RHmin_abs_s + time + (1|branch_ID),
                family=binomial, data=puna_data)

AIC(m_temp, m_rh, m_both)
summary(m_both)
#RESULTADO FINAL #se eligio el modelo Both porque comprarndolos con los solitarios de tmin_abs y RH_min es mejor en que tiene menor AIC
```
comparando modelos 
```{r}
#Escalar clima (muy importante para GLMM)
puna_data <- puna_data %>%
  mutate(
    Tmin_mean_s = scale(Tmin_mean),
    Tmin_abs_s  = scale(Tmin_abs),
    Tmax_mean_s = scale(Tmax_mean),
    RHmin_abs_s = scale(RHmin_abs),
    RHmin_mean_s = scale( RHmin_mean),
    Light_abs_s = scale(Light_abs),
    Light_mean_s = scale(Light_mean)
  )

puna_data$alive <- ifelse(puna_data$dead_alive == "alive", 1, 0)
table(puna_data$alive)

#Modelo con Tmin_mean
m_Tmin <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_mean_s + (1|branch_ID),
                family = binomial,
                data = puna_data,
                control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
#Modelo con Light_mean
m_Light <- glmer(alive ~ Light_mean_s + (1|branch_ID),
                 family = binomial,
                 data = puna_data,
                 control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
#Modelo con RHmin_abs
m_RH <- glmer(alive ~ RHmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
              family = binomial,
              data = puna_data,
              control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
#modelo juntos 
m_full <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_mean_s + Light_mean_s + RHmin_abs_s +
                (1|branch_ID),
                family = binomial,
                data = puna_data,
                control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
summary(m_Tmin)
summary(m_Light)
summary(m_RH)
summary(m_full)
AIC(m_Tmin, m_Light, m_RH, m_full)

```
Evaluando VIF colinearidad
```{r}
#correlacion ENTRE LAS VARIABLES
cor(puna_data[,c("Tmin_mean_s",
                "Tmin_abs_s",
                "Tmax_mean_s",
                "RHmin_mean_s",
                "RHmin_abs_s",
                "Light_abs_s",
                "Light_mean_s")],
    use = "complete.obs")
#SUGIRIO POR CORRELACION SOLO USAR m_Tmin_abs, m_Tmin_mean Y m_RHmin_abs
library(lme4)
m_Tmin_abs <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
                    family = binomial,
                    data = puna_data,
                    control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
m_Tmin_mean <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_mean_s + (1|branch_ID),
                     family = binomial,
                     data = puna_data,
                     control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
m_RHmin_abs <- glmer(alive ~ RHmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
                     family = binomial,
                     data = puna_data,
                     control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
#COMPARAR AIC
AIC(m_Tmin_abs, m_Tmin_mean, m_RHmin_abs)
aic_table <- AIC(m_Tmin_abs, m_Tmin_mean, m_RHmin_abs)
aic_table$delta <- aic_table$AIC - min(aic_table$AIC)
aic_table
summary(m_Tmin_abs)
#MODELO NULO
data_model <- na.omit(puna_data[, c("alive",
                                   "branch_ID",
                                   "Tmin_abs_s",
                                   "Tmin_mean_s",
                                   "RHmin_abs_s")])

m_null <- glmer(alive ~ 1 + (1|branch_ID),
                family = binomial,
                data = data_model,
                control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))

m_Tmin_abs <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
                    family = binomial,
                    data = data_model,
                    control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))

m_Tmin_mean <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_mean_s + (1|branch_ID),
                     family = binomial,
                     data = data_model,
                     control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))

m_RHmin_abs <- glmer(alive ~ RHmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
                     family = binomial,
                     data = data_model,
                     control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))

isSingular(m_null)

AIC(m_null, m_Tmin_abs, m_Tmin_mean, m_RHmin_abs)

##modelo sin branch porque no influye PERO AL NO USAR SE VE QUE ES MEJOR USARLO
m_null_B <- glm(alive ~ 1 ,
                family = binomial,
                data = data_model)

m_Tmin_abs_B <- glm(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s,
                    family = binomial,
                    data = data_model)

m_Tmin_mean_B <- glm(alive ~ Tmin_mean_s ,
                     family = binomial,
                     data = data_model)

m_RHmin_abs_B <- glm(alive ~ RHmin_abs_s ,
                     family = binomial,
                     data = data_model)
AIC(m_null_B, m_Tmin_abs_B, m_Tmin_mean_B, m_RHmin_abs_B)

#VIF (Variance Inflation Factor) mide cuánto aumenta la varianza de un coeficiente debido a colinealidad con otras variables del modelo.
#install.packages("car")
library(car)
m_vif <- glmer(alive ~  Tmin_abs_s + RHmin_abs_s + Tmin_mean + (1|branch_ID),
             family = binomial,
             data = puna_data, 
             control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))
vif(m_vif)

#PROBANDO Temp_min_abs+ rhmin_abs
m_two <- glmer(alive ~ Tmin_abs_s + RHmin_abs_s + (1|branch_ID),
               family = binomial,
               data = data_model,
               control = glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa"))

AIC(m_Tmin_abs, m_two)

```

TEST DE KAPLAN-MEIER o analisis de supervivencia  se usa para estimar y comparar la probabilidad de supervivencia en el tiempo
responde a:
¿Cuál es la probabilidad de supervivencia después de cierto tiempo?
En el bosque, la probabilidad de supervivencia se mantiene en 1 durante todo el experimento.
En la puna, la probabilidad disminuye rápidamente, con una caída abrupta alrededor del mes 5 y una mediana de supervivencia de 5 meses.
¿Son diferentes las probabilidades de supervivencia entre puna vs bosque a lo largo del tiempo? test de log-rank
Sí, son drásticamente diferentes.

```{r}
#paquetes
library(dplyr)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
#preparar datos 
supervivencia <- expdata %>% arrange(epi_ID, time, branch_ID)
supervivencia <- supervivencia %>%
  mutate(
    relocation = as.logical(relocation),
    dead_alive = as.character(dead_alive),
    time_num = as.numeric(gsub("T", "", time))
  )
#crear tabala de supervivencia
surv_data <- supervivencia %>%
  arrange(epi_ID, time_num) %>%
  group_by(epi_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    group = first(relocation),        # TRUE = puna, FALSE = bosque
    event = any(dead_alive == "dead"),
    time_event = ifelse(
      event,
      min(time_num[dead_alive == "dead"]),  # primer dead
      max(time_num)                          # último censo si sigue viva
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#convertir censo a tiempo real 
surv_data <- surv_data %>%
  dplyr:: mutate(
    time_months = dplyr:: recode(
      time_event,
      `1` = 0,
      `2` = 2,
      `3` = 5,
      `4` = 8
    )
  )
table(surv_data$epi_ID, surv_data$time_months)##tabla del evento mortalidad x nepi y time(#mes)
#Crear el objeto de supervivencia
#library(survival)
S <- Surv(
  time  = surv_data$time_months,
  event = surv_data$event
)

#Ajustar Kaplan–Meier por grupo (Puna vs Bosque)
km_fit <- survfit(S ~ group, data = surv_data)
#Log-rank test
survdiff(S ~ group, data = surv_data)

ggsurvplot(
  km_fit,
  data = surv_data,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  pval = TRUE,
  conf.int = FALSE,
  legend.labs = c("Bosque", "Puna"),
  legend.title = "Hábitat",
  palette = c("darkgreen", "brown"),
  linewidth = 1,
  xlab = "Tiempo desde el inicio (meses)",
  ylab = "Probabilidad de supervivencia",
  break.time.by = 1
)
summary(km_fit)#comprobacion rapida

#en ingles
g <- ggsurvplot(
  km_fit,
  data = surv_data,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  pval = TRUE,
  conf.int = FALSE,
  legend.labs = c("Forest", "Puna"),
  legend.title = "Habitat",
  palette = c("darkgreen", "brown"),
  linewidth = 1,
  xlab = "Time since the start (months)",
  ylab = "Survival probability",
  break.time.by = 1
)
png("KaplanMeier_epiphyte_survival.png",
    width = 2000, height = 1600, res = 300)
print(g)


```
Kaplan–Meier survival analysis showed that the probability of survival remained at 1.0 for forest controls throughout the study, whereas epiphytes translocated to the puna experienced a sharp decline in survival probability, with median survival of five months.


grafico de Sobrevivencia
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

names(expdata)

# Ordenar los datos por fecha
supervivencia <- expdata %>% arrange(epi_ID, time, branch_ID)

#grafico supervivencia por rama 
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1️⃣ Preparar los datos
expdata <- expdata %>%
  mutate(
    dead_alive = tolower(dead_alive),   # uniformar texto
    alive = ifelse(dead_alive == "alive", 1, 0),  # 1 = vivo, 0 = muerto
    relocation = as.factor(relocation),
    branch_ID = as.factor(branch_ID),
    time = as.factor(time)
  )

# 2️⃣ Calcular supervivencia promedio (%)
survival_summary <- expdata %>%
  group_by(relocation, branch_ID, time) %>%
  summarise(
    survival = mean(alive, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

##GRAFICO MEJORADO
library(dplyr)
go<- ggplot(
  survival_summary,
  aes(
    x = time,
    y = survival,
    group = branch_ID,
    color = relocation
  )
) +
  geom_line(
    position = position_dodge2(width = 0.3),
    size = 1, alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge2(width = 0.3),
    size = 2, alpha = 0.6
  )+
  facet_wrap(
    ~ relocation,
    ncol = 1,
    labeller = labeller(
      relocation = c(
        "false" = "Puna",
        "true"  = "Forest"
      )
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "false" = "#8B5A2B",  # Puna = marrón
      "true"  = "#1B7837"  # Forest = verde
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "T1" = "April",
      "T2" = "June",
      "T3" = "August",
      "T4" = "December"
    ) 
    )+
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Survival (%) over time by branch",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Survival (%)"
  )
go 

ggsave(
  filename = "survival_plot.png",
  plot = go,
  width = 5,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

```
